libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Use an in-memory embedded database for runtime coverage. If using Typesafe Config/DatabaseConfig, configure the JDBC URL for an in-memory database and set Slick connection pooling to disabled unless the test also intentionally adds slick-hikaricp. Avoid requiring an external PostgreSQL/MySQL/Oracle server or Docker container for the generated tests.

deterministic_setup.reason:
Slick documentation and upstream tests use an embedded in-memory H2 database for the basic getting-started workflow; slick_2.13 itself only depends on Scala, SLF4J API, Typesafe Config, Reactive Streams, Cats Effect, and FS2, so tests cannot execute real JDBC database actions without an added driver
...[truncated]

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #7664 [Automation] java run fails for com.typesafe.slick:slick_2.13:4.0.0-RC1; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: The reported failure is a source/API compatibility compile issue in existing Scala tests, so adding H2 will not by itself fix that compile error.
Slick 4.0.0-RC1 introduced API changes such as the additional MColumn isGenerated parameter; generated or repaired tests must target the new signatures.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Slick is a JDBC/SQL access library and the resolved slick_2.13 artifact does not bring any JDBC database driver; meaningful DBIO/query coverage normally needs an embedded JDBC driver such as H2.
